name: Reeflex Demo 1 - Bulk Delete Guard (real risk, not row count)

nodes.name: When clicking 'Execute workflow'
nodes.notes: Stand-in for whatever actually triggers this (a schedule, a webhook, an agent tool call).

nodes.name: Two realistic bulk-delete requests
nodes.notes: Stand-in for your real upstream data (two queries that found different candidate sets). Replace with your real data source -- keep the same idea: describe each request's TRUE risk axes, do not just vary count.

nodes.name: Reeflex Gate
nodes.notes: One gate node, two input items -> two separate /v1/decide calls (one per item), each with its own session id (execution id + table name) so the two rows don't share a fragmentation budget. See demo1-README.md for the credential setup (preconfigured against the public api-dev eval endpoint).

nodes.name: Execute delete (ALLOWED - recoverable, single, low risk)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - not a real delete. Reached for the stale_carts row: recoverable + single blast radius is genuinely low risk, so reeflex-core allows it.

nodes.name: Would notify approver (HELD - irreversible, broad, production)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real Slack/Email/ticketing node. Reached for the customers row: irreversible + broad + production trips R2 (reeflex.policy/irreversible_broad_prod). This demo stops here -- see demo3-the-approval-loop for what happens after this point (resolve the hold, resubmit, get allow).

nodes.name: Unexpected deny (should not fire in this demo)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - wired for completeness/robustness. Neither sample row should trip R3 (irreversible+systemic+production) -- see demo4-nothing-gets-through for that case.

main.node: Execute delete (ALLOWED - recoverable, single, low risk)
Would notify approver (HELD - irreversible, broad, production)
Unexpected deny (should not fire in this demo)

meta.description: Reeflex n8n demo 1/5 (examples/n8n/, see top-level README.md for the index + credential setup). Teaches: the SAME Reeflex Gate node routes two bulk-delete requests differently based on their TRUE risk axes, not their row count. Two sample rows in one run: a 3-row soft-deletable cleanup (recoverable+single) comes back ALLOWED; a 50-row hard delete of a live table (irreversible+broad+production) comes back HELD FOR APPROVAL. Fully works against the live api-dev eval endpoint -- see demo1-README.md.
